arts.ttl: Julen Lopetegui spells out how Lucas Paqueta can bring himself out of West Ham slump
arts.sum: Brazil star Lucas Paqueta was dropped for last weekend's draw at Fulham but West Ham's head coach underlined the attacking midfielder's importance to his squad after difficult few months

arts.ttl: Maresca already knows Chelsea's next 'top defender'
arts.sum: Speaking to the media ahead of Chelsea's clash with West Ham, the Italian hailed the 18-year-old as 'potentially' the club's next 'top defender' before insisting that he needs to 'moderate' his integration into the first team. Enzo Maresca has hailed Josh Acheampang as Chelsea's next 'top defender' but still refuses to use him.

arts.ttl: 'Easier said than done' - Pundit makes new Lopetegui admission in 'Outstanding' West Ham verdict
arts.sum: Darren Bent has admitted it is going to “take time” for Julen Lopetegui to get the best out of West Ham United’s “outstanding” attacking talent.... The post 'Easier said than done' - Pundit makes new Lopetegui admission in 'Outstanding' West Ham verdict appeared first on West Ham Zone.

arts.ttl: Maresca admits he has 'no idea' about Chelsea's biggest problem
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has admitted that Chelsea 'have no idea' when Reece James will return to full fitness. Despite this, new coach Enzo Maresca did not name a new captain ahead of this season and it is increasingly looking like the doubters were entirely correct.

arts.ttl: 'Find other ways': Gus Poyet has a bit of advice for Chelsea star Cole Palmer this season
arts.sum: Gus Poyet has just delivered an interesting piece of advice to Chelsea talisman Cole Palmer. If there’s one player in the Chelsea squad that came...

arts.ttl: ‘Gifted’ Chelsea player really needs to put in a big performance against West Ham – opinion
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca will likely already have a good idea of the Chelsea team he wants to name when they are next in action this weekend...

arts.ttl: Chelsea’s busy summer means Maresca playing with a stacked deck
arts.sum: Chelsea's summers are almost as busy as their seasons these days - but we're glad all of the nonsense is behind us now and we can focus on what actually matters: the football.... at least until the next international break. With the window closed for almost a month now and even the "bomb squad" largely […]

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca reveals brutal Mykhailo Mudryk Chelsea decision has been made after transfer issues
arts.sum: Even in the 6-2 win against Wolves, Mudryk started the match but saw Chelsea's best attacking play come after the break, once he was replaced by Pedro Neto. He also now has increased competition after Chelsea signed Jadon Sancho, Joao Felix, and Pedro Neto, who are all capable of operating from the left.

arts.ttl: 'Brutal' Boniface shows Arsenal and Chelsea why they need him
arts.sum: Victor Boniface's star continued to rise as one of Europe's most unique strikers put in a brilliant display on his Champions League debut. Boniface retained possession of the ball despite being challenged by two Feyenoord defenders and, after wriggling free, he set up Jeremie Frimpong's cross with a no-look, left-footed pass while feigning a shot.
